samples.xml_description:
Implement a 16-bit divider module, the dividend is 16-bit and the divider is 8-bit in combinational logic. Extract the higher bits of the dividend, matching the bit width of the divisor. Compare these bits with the divisor: if the dividend bits are greater, set the quotient to 1, otherwise set it to 0, and use the difference as the remainder. Concatenate the remainder with the highest remaining 1-bit of the dividend, and repeat the process until all dividend bits are processed. 16-bit dividend. 8-bit divisor. 16-bit quotient. 16-bit remainder. The parameter i represents the current bit position of the dividend being compared with the divisor. The parameter rem represents the remainder obtained by subtracting the divisor from the extracted bits of the dividend.

Divide the input dividend A by the divisor B to obtain the quotient and remainder.

First, initialize two registers a_reg and b_reg with the values of A and B, respectively.

Then, enter a loop that iterates over the bits of the dividend, starting from the highest bit.

For each iteration, extract the highest i bits of the dividend and compare them with the divisor.

If the dividend bits are greater than or equal to the divisor, subtract the divisor from the extracted bits and set the quotient bit at position i to 1.

Otherwise, keep the quotient bit at position i as 0.

Decrement i by 1 and repeat the loop until all bits of the dividend have been processed.

The final value of the remainder is stored in rem.
